QF.id: XC Case Studies and Practical Application_13

question.text: Prakash, age 45, has accumulated Rs.1.5 crore in investments but discovers he has no nominee registered for his mutual fund folios, demat account, or bank accounts. What risk does this create?

answer.text: No risk — his legal heirs will automatically receive everything
His family may face significant delays, legal complications, and administrative hurdles in claiming his assets if something happens to him
SEBI will automatically transfer all assets to his spouse
His investments will be forfeited to the government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Without nominees, legal heirs must go through succession certificate/probate processes for each account — causing delays, legal costs, and administrative burden during an already difficult time. **The Trap:** SEBI doesn't automatically transfer assets. Assets aren't forfeited. But 'automatic' inheritance without nomination involves a lengthy, expensive legal process. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A covers operational aspects including nomination. Series VI covers demat account nomination requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Nomination review should be part of every annual client review — ensure all investment accounts, bank accounts, and insurance policies have updated nominees. Also recommend clients prepare a consolidated asset statement for their families.
